examples: Today under knowledge economy, virtual assets have incontestably become the core competitiveness of enterprise.
Oscar Wilde and Mark Twain both incontestably possessed elegant and witty minds, but no one would mistake either one for his near-contemporary.
After Boris came the colonel and his lady, then the general himself, then the Rostovs, and the soiree now began to be exactly, incontestably, like all other soirees.
Merce Cunningham, incontestably the grand master of modern dance, died two and a half years ago, at the age of ninety.
The Swiss Socialist party "deplored" the result, saying that better protection against passive smoking would have "incontestably been a major step in the improvement of (workers') conditions".
